summary: Russian forces continue using the "Elka" anti-aircraft drone to intercept Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs, with recent kinetic strikes documented. Iranian forces are deploying modified loitering munitions and fiber-optic FPV drones, including attacks on U.S. facilities in Iraq, likely adopting Russian tactics. On the Zaporizhzhia front, a "Dnieper" battalion crew downed multiple Ukrainian drones, including Domaha, Darts, and Chaklun models. Russian army aviation struck a Ukrainian UAV launch site in Kharkiv region using LMUR missiles. Ukrainian forces are employing log-periodic antennas on "Molniyas" to extend range and resist jamming, while also using FPV drones to disrupt Russian infantry, including a documented bayonet charge incident.
